label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to refer back to the passage. The passage mentions that even small bits of space junk, like paint dots, pose a threat because they orbit at speeds up to 28,200 kilometers per hour. It explains that these high speeds cause damage, like breaking windshields of space shuttles so badly they needed replacement. So, the key point here is the speed. The high velocity of the debris, even if it's small, makes it dangerous. So the answer should be related to their speed. Looking at the options: A) They travel at a very high speed. That matches the explanation in the text. B) They are difficult to track by satellites. The passage doesn't mention tracking difficulty as the reason, but rather the speed causing impact damage. So B might not be correct. C) They can blow up into millions of pieces. The text doesn't say the small pieces explode; instead, they are already debris. The problem is their speed, not that they break into more pieces. D) They are as soft as a ball in the air. No, this seems incorrect. The passage
